prs.title: [Model][Hardware][AMD][Kernel]: Enable e2e QK Norm + RoPE + KV Cache runtime fusion for Qwen3-30B-A3B on ROCM_ATTN, ROCM_AITER_FA, and ROCM_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN
prs.author: jhu960213
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

- Enable e2e runtime QK Norm + RoPE + KV Cache fusion for Qwen3-30B. At compile time, we swap the aforementioned fusion pattern & fuse with aiter's high-performance HIP kernel `fused_qk_norm_rope_cache_pts_quant_shuffle ` for all decoder layers that satisfy a preset token compile range guarded by `rope_kvcache_fusion_max_token_num`. 
- This kernel supports writing KV Cache in both shuffle and non-shuffle layouts. ROCM_AITER_FA and ROCM_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN require the fused kernel to write in non-shuffle mode, as this corresponds to the flash layout expected by FA variants. 
- As for ROCM_ATTN, modifications were needed on both the prefill and decode path kernels (prefill Triton, decode Triton 2D for non-power-of-2 block sizes, and decode paged attention ll4 GFX9 variants) so that they can read the value cache in the interleaved mode, which the fused kernel writes in when shuffle=True. The key cache read logic is untouched as the fused kernel already writes in the correct format, which the prefill and decode paths expect.
- When shuffle=True, the fused kernel writes K with shape (blocks, heads, dim/x, block_size, x) and V with shape (blocks, heads, block_size/x, dim, x). Decode paths already natively read K in the same format, but it currently hardcodes reading of the V cache with an expected shape of (blocks, heads, dims, block_size).

## Test Plan

To test this fusion out, please pass the attached toggle through the pass config under the `--compilation-config` when launching with `vllm serve`:

`'pass_config': {
        'fuse_qk_norm_rope_kvcache': True,
        'rope_kvcache_fusion_max_token_num': 256
}`

For example, something like this:

`VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1 \
vllm serve /path-to-your/Qwen3-30B-A3B \
  --host 0.0.0.0 \
  --port 8080 \
  --attention-backend ROCM_ATTN \
  --no-enable-prefix-caching \
  -O1 \
  --compilation-config '{
    "pass_config": {
      "fuse_qk_norm_rope_kvcache": true,
      "rope_kvcache_fusion_ma

prs.title: [ROCm] Rename LLMM1 to vecMatMul, refactor, and fix RDNA4 correctness bugs
prs.author: wjabbour
prs.body_head:
## Summary

Renames `LLMM1`/`LLGemm1_kernel` → `vecMatMul`/`vecMatMul_kernel` across the codebase, extracts helper functions (`load_tile`, `dot_row`, `warp_reduce`), replaces magic numbers with named constants, and adds stage-level comments documenting the four-stage reduction.

Also fixes two silent correctness bugs on RDNA4 (WARP_SIZE=32) that produced wrong results while passing on CDNA CI hardware:

- **Row write bug**: `lane % 32 == 0` hardcoded as the half2 write guard; on WARP_SIZE=32 rows beyond the first pair were never written. Fixed to `lane % (2 * THREADS_PER_ROW_GROUP)`.
- **Cross-warp reduction bug**: reduction loop bounded by `THREADS_PER_ROW_GROUP` instead of `num_warps`, silently dropping warp contributions when K > 2048. Fixed with a strided loop.

Regression tests for both bugs are included, verified red on unfixed code and green on the fix. Tests always pass on CDNA (WARP_SIZE=64) CI hardware — the comments explain why.

Also adds explicit `TORCH_CHECK` guards for `K % 8 == 0`, `M % rows_per_block == 0`, `K <= 8192`, and `rows_per_block ∈ {2, 4, 8, 16}`, and replaces the silent wrong `default` case in the dispatch switch with a hard error.

## Test plan
- `pytest tests/kernels/quantization/test_rocm_skinny_gemms.py -v` on gfx1201 (RDNA4, WARP_SIZE=32)
- 76/76 vecMatMul tests passing; regression tests confirmed red/green on unfixed/fixed code
- Not duplicating any open PR (supersedes #40770, which was opened from main by mistake)
- AI assistance used (Claude); all changed lines reviewed by submitter

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code)

prs.title: [Frontend][RFC] Rust front-end integration
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
See corresponding RFC for introducing a rust-based alternative front-end process in vLLM https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/40846.

For now we have staged the poc implementation in https://github.com/Inferact/vllm-frontend-rs. This PR contains the logic to integrate it into vLLM.

You can try it out by building this branch:

```shell
# Full install
uv pip install .

# Or use pre-compiled wheel
VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED=1 uv pip install --editable .
./build_rust.sh

# Run it
VLLM_USE_RUST_FRONTEND=1 vllm serve ...
```

Co-authored with @BugenZhao

prs.title: [ROCm] Add env flags to disable dynamic MXFP4 quant and enable AITER tuned GEMMs for Attention Projection Layers
prs.author: heachary
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
For models in the DeepSeek family, dynamic MXFP4 quantization is enabled by default for the attention projection layers. This behavior may be suboptimal in certain deployment scenarios.

This PR introduces two opt-in environment variables that provide finer control over this path:

1. The ability to disable dynamic MXFP4 quantization for attention projections.
2. The ability to route unquantized attention GEMMs through AITER's newer FlyDSL-based tuned GEMM implementations, instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

In internal benchmarks, using the FlyDSL GEMM path for unquantized attention projections shows a good performance improvement on models such as Kimi-K2-Thinking.

## Summary
This PR adds two new ROCm environment variables to give operators finer control over quantization and GEMM dispatch for attention linear layers:
- **`VLLM_ROCM_DISABLE_ATTENTION_LINEAR_LAYER_DYNAMIC_MXFP4_QUANT`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, skips the dynamic MXFP4 quantization that is applied to attention projection layers (`q_proj`, `k_proj`, `v_proj`, `o_proj`) which is done upon model load for a certain family of models.
- **`VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_TUNED_UNQUANTISED_GEMM`** (`False` by default) — When enabled, routes unquantized GEMMs in the attention path through AITER's tuned GEMM implementation (`aiter.tuned_gemm.tgemm.mm`) instead of the default skinny-reduce or hipBLASLt paths.

Both flags are opt-in and default to the current behavior, so there is no change for existing users.

## Test Result
### Accuracy
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

Benchmark | Metric | Score | Threshold | Status
-- | -- | -- | -- | --
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (flexible-extract) | 0.944 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS
GSM8K (5-shot, 250 samples) | exact_match (strict-match) | 0.936 | 0.90 | ✅ PASS

### Performance
Model: Kimi-K2-Thinking-MXFP4, TP=4, MI355X, fp8 KV cache

| config | conc | Baseline | AiterTunedFI |

prs.title: [Core][WIP] Check for GPU<->CPU sync during CI
prs.author: njhill
prs.body_head:
vLLM now uses asynchronous scheduling by default and in the majority of cases. Performance relies on the absence of any gpu<->cpu synchronizations on the main cuda stream, but such syncs can be opaque and it is easy for them to creep in accidentally.

This change adds a `VLLM_GPU_SYNC_CHECK` env var which enables `torch.cuda.set_sync_debug_mode` for the model forward pass and sampler, so that we can easily check for such syncs.

I'm trying first to enable it globally in the CI to flush out syncs that need to be fixed or where they are unavoidable and the check needs to be suppressed. Will then probably split the fixes into separate PR(s).

prs.title: [Do Not Merge][Attention] Add FLASH_ATTN_MLA_SPARSE backend
prs.author: MatthewBonanni
prs.body_head:
Requires https://github.com/vllm-project/flash-attention/pull/133, merge that first

FA4 now supports DSA on Blackwell as of https://github.com/Dao-AILab/flash-attention/pull/2441. This PR integrates this support by adding a new `FLASH_ATTN_MLA_SPARSE` backed. Benchmarking is TBD while the PR is in draft -- the priority list will be updated based on the results.

prs.title: [Do Not Merge][Attention] Enable masked MHA for topk sparse attention using FA4
prs.author: MatthewBonanni
prs.body_head:
Requires https://github.com/vllm-project/flash-attention/pull/127, merge that first ## Purpose For pure prefills, there is a sweet spot of sequence length where a masked MHA pathway is faster than sparse MQA. The [DeepSeek V3.2 paper mentions](https://arxiv.org/abs/2512.02556) this: > Note that for short-sequence prefilling, we specially implement a masked MHA mode to simulate DSA, which can achieve higher efficiency under short-context conditions. This PR implements the two optimizations mentioned in https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/31473, point 3: 1. Use un-absorbed dense MHA pathway for requests with `seq_len <= topk` 2. Otherwise, use un-absorbed masked MHA pathway for requests with `min_threshold <= seq_len <= max_threshold` (here, `min_threshold = 128` and `max_threshold = 8192`) In practice, with a typical `topk = 2048`, the routing will be as follows: - `seq_len <= 2048`: dense MHA - `2048 < seq_len <= 8192`: masked MHA - `8192 < seq_len`: sparse MQA ## Test Plan ### Correctness ``` pytest tests/v1/attention/test_sparse_mla_backends.py ``` ### Performance ``` python benchmarks/attention_benchmarks/benchmark.py --config benchmarks/attention_benchmarks/configs/mla_sparse_mha_vs_mqa.yaml ``` ## Test Result ### Correctness Should pass in CI (V1 Attention) ### Performance ---

prs.title: [vLLM IR][Rope] Port RotaryEmbedding and DeepseekScalingRotaryEmbedding to IR Ops
prs.author: wxsIcey
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Port `RotaryEmbedding` and `DeepseekScalingRotaryEmbedding` to vLLM IR, following https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/38756. For the base `RotaryEmbedding`, keep the `key=None` path out of the IR `maybe_inplace` call and fall back to the existing static implementation.

This preserves cross-layer KV sharing behavior and avoids `torch.library` schema inference failures on optional returns.


## Test Plan
To be tested

## Test Result
